janitorial.skip_reason: Duplicate (vertical, legal_name, city, state) within source file: 'Mid-Cities Janitorial' Midland TX appears at both index 50 and 51

notes: All inserts were transactional (CTE-chained INSERT INTO businesses RETURNING id -> INSERT INTO business_scores).
Tier names were already in long form (A_acquire_self/B_forward/C_watch/D_pass) in both source files — no normalization needed.
Layer1 field was named layer1_base_rate in both source files — direct mapping, no fallback to layer1_score required.

String-typed integer fields (employee_count_estimate, owner_age_estimate) were coerced by parsing the leading integer from ranges like '5-15' or '60+'. owner_tenure_years and years_in_business followed the same coercion.
1 record skipped due to in-source duplicate violating offmarket.businesses unique key (vertical, legal_name, city, state).
Painting C_watch count from spec said 21 (matches DB). Janitorial source said C=48 but final DB shows 47 because the one C-tier duplicate (Mid-Cities Janitorial) was deduped.
